title: draba × traunsteineri
intro: Draba × traunsteineri, also known as Traunstein's whitlow grass, is a rare alpine plant species that is a hybrid of Draba crassifolia and Draba intermedia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as an infusion or tincture, often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds, although herbal preparations should be made under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
